fuelwatch_signal: This project provides a reporting and signal board for FuelWatch data, leveraging a Streamlit-based interface for visualization. It solves the problem of presenting fuel data in an accessible and interactive format. The use of Streamlit allows for rapid development of a web-based data application directly from Python scripts.
conformal-stencil-generator: The conformal-stencil-generator project converts 2D SVG vector art into watertight, 3D-printable STL files specifically optimized for flexible materials used in paint masks and airbrushing stencils. It addresses technical challenges like reliable handling of disconnected stencil regions, built-in alignment marks for repeatable masking, and geometry optimized for flat or curved surfaces, enabling rapid and accurate stencil fabrication.
modelviz: ModelViz is a Next.js web application that parses Power BI and Microsoft Fabric semantic model `.zip` archives containing TMDL files and renders interactive diagrams detailing tables, columns, measures, relationships, and security roles. It solves the challenge of comprehensively analyzing and visualizing complex semantic models by providing deterministic parsing, data source classification, and AI-powered natural-language architectural summaries, facilitating easier inspection and secure sharing through obfuscation features. The integration of client-side zip extraction, React Flow-based diagram rendering, and GPT-4.1-mini for model analysis makes it a technically robust tool for semantic model exploration and documentation.
mtd-auto-album: This project, named 'mtd-auto-album', is likely a tool for automatically creating or managing photo albums, possibly for a web-based application given its use of TypeScript. Due to the absence of a description or README, the specific problem it solves or its technical implementation details are not available. The name suggests a focus on automating the process of album creation from a set of images.
print-portfolio: The print-portfolio project is a Next.js web application template designed to help users quickly create and develop a personal portfolio site. It leverages automatic font optimization with the Geist font and provides a streamlined developer experience with hot reloading and easy editing of the main page. This project solves the problem of setting up an efficient, optimized, and performant portfolio frontend using modern Next.js features and best practices.
videoretarder: Videoretarder is a Next.js web application scaffolded with create-next-app that serves as a starting point for developing video-related features or interfaces. Its technical interest lies primarily in leveraging Next.js for optimized web performance, including the automatic loading and optimization of the Geist font via next/font, facilitating fast, modern React-based development with built-in support for incremental updates and easy deployment on Vercel.
invoicepipe: InvoicePipe is a full-stack TypeScript application designed for automated ingestion and processing of invoices, leveraging Azure Document Intelligence for smart field extraction and delivering a tenant-isolated, secure, and responsive UI built with Next.js and Tailwind CSS. Technically, it solves the challenge of reliably extracting structured data from diverse Australian invoices through a custom normalization pipeline and ensures data integrity with features like checksum deduplication, typed API routes, and secure authentication via NextAuth integrated with Prisma ORM. The system facilitates seamless previewing and exporting of extracted metadata in JSON or CSV formats, streamlining integration with accounting or downstream processing pipelines.
ftl: This project provides a visual guide to 3D printing filaments, organizing them into tiers to help makers select the best material for their needs. It features an interactive interface built with Next.js and Tailwind CSS, allowing users to click on filament cards to view detailed information, including pros, cons, and use cases. The project solves the problem of filament selection by presenting a clear, comparative, and user-friendly resource.
drawtalk: DrawTalk is a collaborative drawing application enabling users to communicate solely through drawings, enhanced by a novel three-layer stroke system for nuanced visual expression. It integrates large language model (LLM) vision capabilities to interpret and interact with the sketches, bridging visual input with AI understanding. This approach addresses the challenge of seamless AI-human interaction within purely visual communication mediums.
kitforge: KitForge is a web application that enables users to upload and analyze 3D printing files (STL, 3MF, OBJ) to extract geometric properties, estimate material costs and print times, and generate detailed kit cards in multiple formats. It leverages FastAPI and Streamlit for a responsive user experience, combined with the trimesh library for advanced mesh analysis such as watertightness and multi-shell detection. This project addresses the challenge of accurately quantifying 3D print parameters and presenting professional summaries, streamlining the preparation process for 3D printing projects.
PBN: This project allows users to generate paint-by-number images from any input image, vectorizing them into SVG format. It utilizes k-means clustering to quantize the image to a specified number of colors, which can be restricted to a user-defined palette. The project also provides a command-line interface for batch processing and customization of the generation process.
take-the-wheel: Take The Wheel is a Chrome extension that enables browser-based agents to authenticate and securely retrieve user cookies for Robotic Process Automation (RPA) and automated browser actions. It solves the problem of safely granting automated scripts authorized access to browser cookies by using token-based authentication with configurable expirations, domain-specific filtering, and a native messaging interface for integration with external applications. Its inclusion of a Python client library and adherence to standard cookie formats make it technically appealing for seamless, secure credential management and automation workflows.
pixeltovoxel-demo: Pixeltovoxel-demo is a multi-camera motion capture system that reconstructs 3D voxel representations from synchronized video streams, providing an interactive web-based visualization interface. Technically, it integrates a high-performance C++ ray-casting voxel reconstruction pipeline with a scalable architecture consisting of a React/Next.js frontend, an Express backend with tRPC API, and a FastAPI compute node that handles multi-camera capture and processing via OpenCV. This setup addresses the challenge of real-time volumetric motion capture by combining advanced computer vision algorithms, efficient multi-stream data synchronization, and intuitive 3D rendering within a web application.
flow: Next.js Commerce is a high-performance, server-rendered e-commerce application built with Next.js App Router that leverages modern React features such as Server Components, Server Actions, Suspense, and useOptimistic for optimized user experience and developer ergonomics. It specifically addresses the complexity of integrating with various commerce backends by providing a flexible template that can easily swap out provider implementations, like Shopify, while maintaining consistent functionality and performance. This modular approach simplifies building scalable e-commerce solutions with cutting-edge React and Next.js capabilities.
swipehire: SwipeHire is a job search platform that uses a Tinder-like swipe interface combined with AI to match candidates with job opportunities. It leverages large language model (LLM) analysis to provide instant qualification feedback and improve matching accuracy based on users' profiles, preferences, and uploaded resumes. This approach simplifies job hunting by integrating intuitive swiping gestures with advanced AI-driven job recommendations and application tracking.
print-cost-evaluator: The 3D Print Cost Evaluator is a Streamlit web application designed to provide detailed cost estimations for 3D printed models by accounting for material, energy, labor, and automation factors. It enables users to input specific model parameters and environmental preferences to receive a comprehensive breakdown of true printing costs, profit margins, and whether a print job can run unattended. This tool addresses the complexity of accurately pricing 3D prints by integrating multiple cost drivers beyond just filament consumption, supporting better pricing decisions in additive manufacturing workflows.
mercy-az-ocr: The mercy-az-ocr project provides a FastAPI-based microservice that automates invoice processing by leveraging Azure Content Understanding to extract structured metadata from PDF invoices. It solves the challenge of transforming unstructured invoice documents into accessible, machine-readable data through a containerized service with exposed API endpoints for integration. The project’s seamless deployment via Docker and Azure Container Apps alongside automated CI/CD scripts makes it technically robust for scalable, cloud-native invoice automation workflows.
keychain-maker: The Keychain Maker project is a Streamlit web app that generates customized OpenSCAD keychain files by combining user-provided text and fonts with OpenSCAD templates. It automates the creation of ready-to-print `.scad` and optionally `.stl` files, including support for multi-color STL outputs optimized for dual-color 3D printing. This streamlines keychain customization and 3D print preparation by integrating font embedding, template processing, and slicer-ready multi-material design in a single user-friendly interface.
kitforge-military: This project is an e-commerce prototype for selling 3D-printed military kit cards, built with Next.js 15. It features a Three.js-based 3D model viewer that allows users to preview products with custom liveries. The application solves the problem of visualizing customized 3D-printed products online by providing an interactive and responsive user experience.
hyperbolic_paraboloid_generator: The hyperbolic_paraboloid_generator project creates precise 3D models of hyperbolic paraboloid surfaces, which are saddle-shaped structures often used in architectural and engineering designs. By automating the generation of these complex curved geometries, it facilitates rapid prototyping and analysis of doubly ruled surfaces that are mathematically challenging to model manually. This tool addresses the technical difficulty of parametrizing and visualizing hyperbolic paraboloids for practical applications in design and structural optimization.
Pixeltovoxelprojector: Pixeltovoxelprojector detects pixel-level motion in image sequences and projects these changes into a 3D voxel grid for detailed spatial and temporal analysis. It addresses the challenge of translating 2D motion data into 3D representations by combining a high-performance C++ backend for intensive image processing with a Python frontend for visualization and coordinate transformations. This architecture enables precise motion tracking and visualization, particularly useful in fields like astronomy and surveillance where understanding motion in three dimensions is critical.
untap: untap is a real-time synthetic monitoring platform that performs health checks on over 20 types of services every 60 seconds, detecting incidents through a rolling failure rate algorithm with hysteresis thresholds. It addresses the challenge of timely and reliable service status tracking by combining frequent automated probes with an elegant dark-themed dashboard that organizes services into categories and provides incident timelines and automatic owner notifications. The platform’s integration of diverse monitoring protocols (HTTP, TCP, ICMP), coupled with sophisticated incident detection logic and a responsive UI, enables proactive awareness and rapid response to service disruptions.
WaveFunctionCollapse: WaveFunctionCollapse is a procedural content generation tool that creates new bitmaps or tilemaps exhibiting local pattern similarities to a given example input. It uses an algorithm inspired by quantum mechanics, representing pixel states as superpositions and iteratively collapsing these states based on Shannon entropy and pattern frequency to ensure output consistency with the input’s local patterns. This approach solves the complex problem of generating diverse yet coherent outputs that maintain the statistical distribution and adjacency rules of input textures, enabling applications in game development and texture synthesis.
MarkovJunior: MarkovJunior is a probabilistic programming language that models computation through ordered rewrite rules applied on patterns, with inference achieved via constraint propagation. It enables the generation of complex stochastic processes, such as maze backtracking and puzzle solving, by selectively applying rewrite rules that transform grid states while satisfying specified future constraints. This approach uniquely combines Markov-style rule rewriting with probabilistic inference, allowing the creation of diverse, constraint-driven simulations and procedural content generation models.
gsap-parallax-scroll: The gsap-parallax-scroll project is a Next.js application that likely demonstrates parallax scrolling effects using the GreenSock Animation Platform (GSAP). It provides a live development environment to build and preview interactive, smoothly animated scroll-based UI elements. This project addresses the challenge of integrating performant, visually appealing parallax animations within a modern React framework like Next.js.
linkedin-post-generator: This project transforms GitHub repositories into professional LinkedIn posts using AI, analyzing code to generate insights and publish-ready content. It is technically interesting for its use of multi-layer PII redaction, which combines regex, dictionary replacements, and AI semantic analysis to ensure privacy. The application also features commit graph visualizations and AI-generated images to enrich the posts.
video-agent-v1: video-agent-v1 is a tool that ingests YouTube videos, extracts frames and transcripts, and enables semantic search and LLM-powered question answering over video content. It handles videos with or without transcripts by applying a vision-based fallback using increased frame sampling and supports evidence retrieval via FAISS vector indexing. This project addresses the challenge of extracting searchable, queryable knowledge from video media by combining deterministic frame sampling, transcript analysis, and large language models into an asynchronous REST API service.
infinite-scroll-gsap: The project implements an infinite scrolling interface using Next.js, enhanced with GSAP (GreenSock Animation Platform) for smooth, performant animations. It solves the technical challenge of integrating continuous content loading with advanced animation effects in a React-based framework, optimizing user experience and application responsiveness.
video-agent-main: The video-agent-main project likely implements an autonomous agent designed to interact with or analyze video content. video-agent: Video-Agent is a research-focused system that processes YouTube videos by extracting deterministic frames, generating labeled collages, indexing transcripts with semantic search, and enabling LLM-powered question answering about video content. Technically, it integrates computer vision and natural language processing by linking visual data and transcript embeddings through FAISS indexing, allowing efficient retrieval of video segments and frames in response to queries. Its asynchronous ingestion pipeline and REST API facilitate scalable, non-blocking video analysis and interactive exploration using large language models.
vehicle-affordability-finder: This project provides a Streamlit application that acts as a smart vehicle affordability calculator, helping users determine a realistic car budget by applying the 20/4/10 rule and personalized financial modeling. It stands out by offering actionable recommendations if a vehicle is unaffordable and featuring an extensible design for future integration with real-time inventory APIs. The system's ability to model user discipline and income volatility adds a layer of sophisticated, personalized financial guidance.
convergent: This project, convergent, is a simulation that explores how multiple AI agents form consensus through structured conversation. It visualizes the agents' semantic similarity as a flocking simulation, where agents converge in 3D space as their conceptual alignment increases. The technical interest lies in its use of multiple AI models (GPT-4o, Claude-3.5, Gemini 1.5 Pro) and the real-time visualization of their semantic convergence using React-Three-Fiber.
openai-key-guardian: OpenAI-Key-Guardian is a GitHub scanning tool designed to detect exposed OpenAI API keys and similar secrets within public repositories. It supports multiple operational modes, including a safe data-collection mode, automated issue creation to notify repository owners, and a remediation mode that can fork repositories and submit pull requests with security notices. This project addresses the problem of accidental secret leakage by enabling proactive detection and response workflows, balancing automation with ethical considerations and user permissions.

expressive-state-mapper: Expressive State Mapper (ESM) is a system that captures and analyzes vector drawings enriched with self-notes, extracting approximately 25 objective features related to geometry, color, and drawing dynamics to track deviations from a user's personal baseline over time. Technically, it uniquely combines fine-grained stroke-level data capture with longitudinal within-person analysis using z-score baselines, prioritizing privacy and avoiding subjective interpretation by focusing strictly on measurable signals rather than psychological labels. The integration of a TypeScript Next.js frontend with Python-powered feature extraction and PostgreSQL persistence enables real-time data capture, robust feature computation, and dynamic visualization of individual drawing trajectories.
databricks-lakehouse-pipeline: This project provides an end-to-end demonstration of a retail data pipeline using an Azure Databricks Lakehouse architecture. It processes data through Bronze, Silver, and Gold layers, transforming raw data into aggregated business metrics. The use of PySpark, Delta Lake, and SQL for incremental ETL, data validation, and cleansing showcases a modern approach to building scalable and reliable data analytics solutions on the Databricks platform.
closegate: Closegate is a Next.js application that generates professional, AI-enhanced memes tailored for LinkedIn users, leveraging OpenAI's DALL-E 2 API to create polished corporate-style meme images from popular templates. It addresses the niche challenge of producing engaging yet professional meme content suitable for LinkedIn by offering customizable captions with flexible positioning and incorporating prompt injection protection to ensure secure and appropriate content generation. The project stands out by combining modern web technologies with AI-driven image editing, delivering a fast, responsive, and secure meme creation tool specifically designed for startup founders and corporate content creators.
Big-jpg: This repository serves as a portfolio hub showcasing several technically diverse projects including AI-based multi-agent reasoning, generative text visualization, Newtonian orbital simulation, neural network experiments, 2D-to-3D image projections, and automated document workflows. The projects demonstrate advanced applications of AI, physics simulation, and spatial reconstruction, leveraging technologies like Next.js, Python, PyTorch, and Azure to solve complex problems such as emergent agent behavior modeling, spatial data transformation, and intelligent invoice processing.

orbitme: Orbitme is a real-time 3D solar system simulator that models Newtonian n-body gravitational physics with dual integrators (Symplectic Euler and RK4) for balancing speed and accuracy. It visualizes planetary orbits using Three.js and React Three Fiber, offering interactive controls to adjust simulation parameters such as time scale, mass scaling, and integration method. By simulating accurate gravitational interactions between celestial bodies with configurable physical parameters, orbitme enables detailed exploration of orbital dynamics and gravitational effects in a visually immersive environment.
NeuralNet: NeuralNet implements a multilayer perceptron (MLP) neural network entirely from scratch using vanilla JavaScript, supporting modular architectures, various activation functions (sigmoid, ReLU), backpropagation, and training on datasets like XOR, Iris, and MNIST. Its technical significance lies in providing a transparent, educational framework that manually handles forward and backward propagation, gradient calculations, and optimization techniques—demonstrated by achieving up to 96.5% accuracy on MNIST without relying on external libraries. This project solves the problem of understanding deep learning internals by exposing and debugging each component in pure code.
recupare: Recupare integrates InvoicePipe’s Azure Content Understanding OCR capabilities into a secure, user-authenticated platform built with Stack Auth and Neon database. It automates invoice processing by extracting structured data such as vendor details, amounts, and purchase order numbers from PDFs while ensuring data isolation per user. This combination addresses the challenge of securely managing invoice workflows with real-time processing and unified data storage in a responsive, modern web interface.

It integrates with Next.js projects through a CLI that simplifies setup, dependency management, and component installation, addressing the complexity of building AI-focused interfaces with streamlined tooling and ready-made elements.
swaptube: SwapTube is a custom video rendering tool that builds on FFMPEG for encoding but implements most higher-level video and audio processing functions independently. It supports advanced features such as CUDA-accelerated simulations, 3D graphics using GLM, and in-video LaTeX equation rendering via MicroTeX, enabling the creation of specialized and computationally intensive video content. This approach addresses the challenge of producing complex, custom-rendered YouTube videos without relying heavily on standard graphics libraries.
on-prem-to-cloud-data-lineage: The On-Prem to Cloud Data Lineage project is a full-stack TypeScript web application that visualizes and traces detailed data lineage across on-premise and cloud environments. It offers real-time, column-level metadata insights and interactive Mermaid.js visualizations to track data transformations from raw datasets through curated layers, helping data engineers and stewards verify data flows accurately. roundtable: Roundtable enables multi-participant conversations by orchestrating multiple AI models and human contributors within a single real-time dialogue using the Vercel AI Gateway. openai-agents-js: The openai-agents-js project provides a lightweight JavaScript/TypeScript framework for building and orchestrating multi-agent workflows with LLMs that can interact dynamically through handoffs and tool integrations. It enables developers to create complex, safe, and observable agent systems by offering features like schema-validated outputs, streaming responses, parallel execution, built-in tracing, and configurable guardrails for input/output validation. This framework solves the challenge of managing and debugging collaborative AI agents in real-time workflows, making it easier to build scalable, reliable voice and multi-agent applications.
pyscraper: The pyscraper project implements an asynchronous PDF processing pipeline that intelligently segments PDFs based on content structure, performs OCR extraction using multiple providers (OpenAI, Azure, Google), and exports the extracted text into various formats including HTML, Markdown, JSON, CSV, Excel, and plain text. It addresses challenges in scalable PDF text extraction by combining content-aware chunking with robust async processing, error handling, and progress tracking to efficiently handle large or complex documents. The integration of configurable OCR backends and real-time CLI feedback makes it suitable for automated workflows requiring flexible and reliable PDF text extraction.
crud-webapp-review-sql: The Data Lineage Tracker is a Next.js application designed to visualize and track data transformations across multiple layers of an ETL pipeline, from on-premises SQL Server sources through bronze, silver, and gold lakehouse stages to semantic Power BI models. webappanalyzer: Webappanalyzer is an open-source continuation of the Wappalyzer project that detects web technologies by analyzing webpage elements such as HTML code, JavaScript variables, cookies, response headers, and DNS records using a comprehensive set of regular expressions. It maintains the original JSON-based fingerprint structure to identify technologies without altering user experience, solving the problem of preserving technology detection capabilities in an open, community-driven format after Wappalyzer went private. This approach enables developers and researchers to programmatically recognize and categorize web technologies efficiently through standardized pattern matching.
taxti: taxti is a Streamlit app designed to help Australian freelancers, consultants, and small business owners estimate and log their 2024-25 tax deductions for business-related subscription services. The app also supports data export and session persistence, enhancing accuracy and convenience for tax preparation.
